P1 (Critical): We are encountering an emergency issue, which means that the system or database is not available and productivity has been halted. The product is unusable in its current state. XXX will acknowledge and begin troubleshooting P1 tickets within 15 minutes. P1 refers to a catastrophic failure that has rendered the entire business system landscape unusable and inaccessible for at least one business function or division. This is always considered a Major Incident.
P2 (High): We are encountering a major issue, which means that the system or database is available, but experiencing issues that have an impact on productivity. XXX will acknowledge and begin troubleshooting P2 tickets within 60-120 minutes. P2 refers to a failure or outage resulting in some part of the system being inaccessible. Some functionality may still be available but most elements are missing or not working. There might be some alternative ways to access features but it is difficult. This may be considered a Major Incident depending on impact and urgency.
P3 (Medium): Non-emergency requests. These are operational tasks that take less than 30 minutes to complete XXX will acknowledge and begin action within 4 hours during normal business hours. Moderate business Impact, moderate loss of services. P3 refers to a segment of internal customers facing errors or issues making their work difficult but not impossible.
P4 (Low): Non-emergency requests. These are operational tasks that take less than 30 minutes to complete XXX will acknowledge and begin action within 8 hours during normal business hours. Moderate business Impact, moderate loss of services. P4refers to iIssues that don’t impact the systems overall functioning but may be causing issues for internal or external customers. Doesn’t require an all-hands on deck approach but should be fixed. This may include issues where the usability isn’t impacted dramatically but there are small issues that should be fixed with time permits (Low Urgency, Low Impact).

P1 (Critical): Entire core of applications or functions

• First call response –Within 15 minutes (min) • Acknowledge Issue, Provide timeline for the solution and fixes. • Rapid escalation to XX and team engagement, if unable to resolve within the 1st hour. • Notification to senior executives • Provide ETA or likely causes and troubleshooting / findings • Root Cause Analysis
P2 (High): • First call response –Within 2 hours (min) • Acknowledge Issue, Provide timeline for the solution and fixes. • Standard escalation to XX and team engagement, if unable to resolve within 4 hours. • Notification to IT managers • Provide ETA or likely causes and troubleshooting / findings • Working with change control team. • Root Cause Analysis
P3 (Medium): • First call response –Within 4 hours (min) • Acknowledge Issue, Provide timeline for the solution and fixes. • Standard escalation to XX and team engagement, if unable to resolve within 24 hours. • Notification to IT managers • Continuous support during regular business hours. • Working with change control team.
P4 (Low): • First call response –Within 8 hours (min) • Acknowledge Issue, Provide timeline for the solution and fixes. • Continuous support during regular business hours. • Working with change control team.

P1 (Critical): Total number of P1 tickets that met the P1 SLO definition / total number of P1 tickets.
P2 (High): Total number of P2 tickets that met the P2 SLO definition / total number of P2 tickets.
P3 (Medium): Total number of P3 tickets that met the P3 SLO definition / total number of P3 tickets.
P4 (Low): Total number of P41 tickets that met the P4 SLO definition / total number of P4 tickets.
